text: ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Al Mayadeen: Hezbollah MP Hassan Fadlallah said fighters are surprising the "enemy" with strikes from inside and outside Bint Jbeil, and that withdrawal is not in their dictionary. He stated that Israel is only seeking an image and remains unable to plant its feet in front-line villages despite alleged "massacres against civilians." He criticised the Lebanese government for making concessions to the "enemy" and failing to appreciate the resistance, calling for a comprehensive ceasefire and full implementation of the previous agreement. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Al Mayadeen: A senior Iranian political‑security source told the Lebanese network that a ceasefire in Lebanon will take effect from tonight. The truce will last one week, extending until the end of the ceasefire between Iran and the United States. The source added that Netanyahu may act as a spoiler and attempt to undermine it. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 A member of the Assembly of Experts’ presidium, Ayatollah Araki, said that the vote for Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader was the highest, with a very large margin over other candidates. Members of the Assembly were invited to Qom for several days of consultations and research before the session. When the session convened, all members voted with clarity and insight, and Khamenei’s vote was the highest by a wide margin. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel A senior U.S. delegation visited Somaliland yesterday. The commander of U.S. Africa Command met with Somaliland's chief of staff. Somaliland officials said after the meeting that "control near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait and the Red Sea would significantly change the U.S. approach to dealing with the Houthis and Iran." Somaliland expressed support for the U.S. and Israel using its territory against the Houthis. This comes a day after Israeli reports of concern that the Houthis may attempt to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. N12. tweet

Open Source Intel Important to note: mixed signals aren’t a contradiction. They reflect parallel contingency planning on both sides. There is a lot of mixed reporting and conflicting signals about ceasefire and continuation in Iran right now. Here’s the situation: 1. U.S. officials preparing for potential escalation, including possible ground operations inside Iran (The Washington Post) 2. Iran warns it will treat continued U.S. blockade as a ceasefire breach and move to halt regional trade with force (Iranian official statements) 3. U.S. deploying thousands of additional troops to the Middle East (The Washington Post) 4. Iran threatens to disrupt Red Sea shipping if blockade continues (Iranian military spokesperson) 5. Israel says ending the campaign depends on Iran giving up enriched uranium (Israeli Defense Ministry) 6. Trump: no deal unless Iran gives up nuclear weapons (Fox News) 7. Trump signals ceasefire may not be extended (ABC News) 8. Mediators nearing a deal to extend the U.S.-Iran ceasefire (Associated Press) 9. Iran FM says U.S. must stop Israeli actions in Lebanon for ceasefire to materialize (Iranian Foreign Ministry) 10. Trump says war is “close to over” and Iran wants a deal (Fox News) - Open Source Intel tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇪🇺🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed a European plan to open the Strait of Hormuz without U.S. involvement, saying: "We're doing it now — the ships are coming back and forth," and questioning how Europe could take such a position after relying on…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 U.S. President Donald Trump said he will fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell if he does not leave "on time," stating: "I've held back firing him. I've wanted to fire him." Trump linked the threat to an ongoing probe into the cost of a Federal Reserve building renovation, which he said was projected at $25 million but may reach $4 billion, saying he would not drop the investigation. "Whether it's incompetence, corruption or both, I think you have to find out." Trump also said he believes his preferred Fed Chair nominee Kevin Walsh would be "great," and suggested that outgoing Senator Tom Tillis — who he said "quit" — would not want the legacy of blocking the nomination. @wfwitness

Trump also reiterated his view that the U.S. should control Greenland to counter Russia and China, and said the country should not be "paying trillions of dollars to NATO." @wfwitness
